prompt:
We publish a little shelf-label SDK for the warehouse printers, and last release we deprecated the old entry points (the string-soup factory, RenderRaw, the old Checksum name) in favour of the spec-object API. Downstream teams got the [Obsolete] warnings; what we forgot is that our OWN sheet builder still calls all the old stuff, and our CI compiles with warnings as errors plus the IDE style analyzers enforced in-build. So now:

    SheetBuilder.cs(13,24): error CS0618: 'LabelSpec.Make(string, string, int)' is obsolete: 'build a LabelSpec with object-initializer properties instead'
    SheetBuilder.cs(21,12): error CS0618: 'Renderer.RenderRaw(string, string, int)' is obsolete: 'use Render(LabelSpec)'
    SheetBuilder.cs(25,30): error CS0618: 'LabelCodes.Checksum(string)' is obsolete: 'use Mod10'
    SheetBuilder.cs(28,70): error IDE0060: Remove unused parameter 'binHint' if it is not part of a shipped public API
    SheetBuilder.cs(34,63): error IDE0060: Remove unused parameter 'operatorName' (https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/fundamentals/code-analysis/style-rules/ide0060)
    LabelKitTests.cs(70,51): error CS7036: There is no argument given that corresponds to the required parameter 'binHint' of 'SheetBuilder.AisleHeader(string, int, string)'
    LabelKitTests.cs(77,53): error CS7036: There is no argument given that corresponds to the required parameter 'operatorName' of 'SheetBuilder.SheetFooter(int, string)'

Ground rules: the [Obsolete] members themselves STAY — downstream code still compiles against them and we keep them until the next major. It's our call sites that move to the replacements. The two dead parameters date from the bin-hint and operator-stamp features we cancelled; the test suite was already written against the cleaned-up shapes (that's the CS7036 noise), so take the parameters out rather than parking them under a discard name. Zero behavior change to any rendered line, checksum or sheet — the xunit suite pins every string. No #pragma warning, no [SuppressMessage], no editing LabelKitTests.cs, LabelKit.csproj, .editorconfig or .gitignore. Done when `dotnet test` is green.
notes: 5W4 item 6: obsolete-API migration + IDE-level unused-parameter cleanup promoted by -warnaserror. Authoring toolchain: dotnet SDK 10.0.302. Gate mechanics per catalog: TreatWarningsAsErrors + EnforceCodeStyleInBuild in the protected csproj, with the protected .editorconfig setting dotnet_diagnostic.IDE0060.severity=warning and dotnet_code_quality_unused_parameters=all (probed: IDE0060 then fires in dotnet build/test even on public methods and is promoted to error). Planted: CS0618 x3 across SheetBuilder call sites, one per obsolete-member shape (static factory LabelSpec.Make -> object initializer with required/init props, Renderer.RenderRaw -> Render(LabelSpec), LabelCodes.Checksum -> Mod10); the [Obsolete] members must REMAIN (prompt: downstream compatibility) and their bodies delegate to the new API, which Roslyn does not warn on. IDE0060 x2: cancelled-feature params binHint (public AisleHeader) and operatorName (internal SheetFooter, callable by the same-assembly tests). The frozen tests already call the post-cleanup 2-arg/1-arg signatures, so pre-fix they produce CS7036 — deliberate, cpp-fairride precedent: it pins parameter REMOVAL as the only passing fix (a '_' rename would silence IDE0060 but leave the arity wrong), and the dump lines are included in the prompt as the user's own CI output. Library semantics identical pre/post on every path; tests pin exact rendered strings (pad/truncate/exact-width vs Width 24 default), Luhn-style Mod10 values verified by hand (7141->5, 88->5, 6->4, no digits->0), sheet building incl. trimmed stock lines, check lines, headers, footer. Domain check: no existing label/SKU-rendering seed (csharp-microfiche/wiredepot etc. are other domains); ts-invoice-render is a different lang/kind/surface.
